diagnoses.name: Coronary artery disease s/p aortocoronary bypass graft
diagnoses.evidence_span: prior coronary artery disease s/p aortocoronary bypass graft

diagnoses.name: Multiple myeloma not having achieved remission
diagnoses.evidence_span: multiple myeloma not having achieved remission with associated monoclonal gammopathy

diagnoses.name: Acute pulmonary embolism without acute cor pulmonale
diagnoses.evidence_span: acute pulmonary embolism without acute cor pulmonale

diagnoses.name: Deep venous thrombosis of left femoral vein and left popliteal vein
diagnoses.evidence_span: deep venous thrombosis involving the left femoral vein and left popliteal vein

diagnoses.name: Ventricular tachycardia
diagnoses.evidence_span: developed ventricular tachycardia that required urgent rate and rhythm control

diagnoses.name: Secondary pulmonary hypertension
diagnoses.evidence_span: secondary pulmonary hypertension, likely multifactorial in etiology related to his cardiac and thromboembolic disease

diagnoses.name: Hypokalemia
diagnoses.evidence_span: Electrolyte abnormalities were identified and treated aggressively, including hypokalemia, hypomagnesemia, and hypophosphatemia

diagnoses.name: Hypomagnesemia
diagnoses.evidence_span: Electrolyte abnormalities were identified and treated aggressively, including hypokalemia, hypomagnesemia, and hypophosphatemia

diagnoses.name: Hypophosphatemia
diagnoses.evidence_span: Electrolyte abnormalities were identified and treated aggressively, including hypokalemia, hypomagnesemia, and hypophosphatemia

diagnoses.name: History of falling
diagnoses.evidence_span: A history of falling was documented in his record

procedures.name: Anticoagulation with intravenous heparin
procedures.evidence_span: Anticoagulation was initiated and optimized with intravenous heparin infusions at varying doses (800-1500 units/hour)

procedures.name: Pneumococcal 23-valent polysaccharide vaccine
procedures.evidence_span: the patient received pneumococcal 23-valent polysaccharide vaccine

procedures.name: Influenza vaccine quadrivalent
procedures.evidence_span: the patient received pneumococcal 23-valent polysaccharide vaccine and influenza vaccine quadrivalent

medications.name: Heparin
medications.evidence_span: intravenous heparin infusions at varying doses (800-1500 units/hour)

medications.name: Metoprolol tartrate
medications.dosage: 25 mg initially, titrated to 12.5 mg and 2.5 mg
medications.evidence_span: metoprolol tartrate initiated at 25 mg BID, titrated down to 12.5 mg and 2.5 mg IV dosing

medications.name: Digoxin
medications.dosage: 0.5 mg and 0.25 mg IV; 0.125 mg PO
medications.evidence_span: Digoxin was administered both IV (0.5 mg and 0.25 mg doses) and PO (0.125 mg daily)

medications.name: Senna
medications.evidence_span: Senna was also provided for constipation prophylaxis

medications.name: Magnesium sulfate
medications.evidence_span: Magnesium sulfate was administered IV at 2 gm and 4 gm doses

medications.name: Phosphorus supplementation
medications.evidence_span: Phosphorus supplementation was given both IV and PO (250 mg and 500 mg PO doses)

medications.name: Potassium chloride
medications.evidence_span: Potassium chloride replacement was administered in critical care dosing regimens, with multiple supplementation rounds (40 mEq, 60 mEq, and 80 mEq) along with standard 20 mEq and 40 mEq doses PO

medications.name: IV calcium gluconate
medications.evidence_span: Calcium supplementation via IV calcium gluconate was administered to stabilize the cardiac membrane

medications.name: Normal saline
medications.evidence_span: The patient received IV fluid resuscitation with normal saline

medications.name: Dextrose-containing solutions
medications.evidence_span: The patient received IV fluid resuscitation with normal saline and dextrose-containing solutions
